_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-18 16:03:36Z)**_

This is going to be long — but you asked for it end-to-end. I'll structure it in 4 parts: **Startup**, **Per-message request flow**, **Background jobs**, **Edge cases**. Every step shows which storage is touched and why.

---

# PART 1: Startup / Initialization (One-Time)

When your Bun app boots, you initialize all four systems before serving traffic.

## 1.1 SQLite Schema

Open a single Bun SQLite file (e.g., `memoryos.db`). Run migrations to create:

```sql
-- Users
CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- STM
CREATE TABLE stm_pages (
  page_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  query TEXT NOT NULL,
  response TEXT NOT NULL,
  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
  chain_id TEXT,          -- groups pages into a chain
  meta_chain TEXT,        -- LLM summary of the chain (filled async)
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_stm_user_time ON stm_pages(user_id, timestamp);

-- MTM Segments
CREATE TABLE mtm_segments (
  segment_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  summary TEXT NOT NULL,
  keywords TEXT NOT NULL,           -- JSON array
  n_visit INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  l_interaction INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  last_access_time INTEGER NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_mtm_user ON mtm_segments(user_id);

-- MTM Pages
CREATE TABLE mtm_pages (
  page_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  segment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  query TEXT NOT NULL,
  response TEXT NOT NULL,
  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
  meta_chain TEXT,
  keywords TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (segment_id) REFERENCES mtm_segments(segment_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_mtm_pages_segment ON mtm_pages(segment_id);

-- LPM static profiles
CREATE TABLE user_profile (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT, gender TEXT, birth_year INTEGER,
  extras TEXT  -- JSON
);
CREATE TABLE agent_profile (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  role TEXT, character TEXT,
  extras TEXT
);

-- LPM dynamic — User Traits (90 dims)
CREATE TABLE user_traits (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  traits TEXT NOT NULL  -- JSON: {"fitness_motivation": 0.9, ...}
);

-- LPM dynamic — User KB (FIFO 100)
CREATE TABLE user_kb (
  entry_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  fact TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_kb_user_time ON user_kb(user_id, created_at);

-- LPM dynamic — Agent Traits (FIFO 100)
CREATE TABLE agent_traits (
  entry_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  trait TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_traits_user_time ON agent_traits(user_id, created_at);
```

## 1.2 Qdrant Collections

Create four collections (vector_dim depends on your embedder, e.g., 1536 for OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small`).

```
mtm_segments
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)

mtm_pages
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload indexes: user_id (keyword), segment_id (keyword)

lpm_user_kb
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)

lpm_agent_traits
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)
```

No collection for STM (only 7 pages, retrieved as a whole).

## 1.3 ioredis + BullMQ Setup

Spin up one Redis connection. Define queues:

```ts
const connection = new IORedis({ maxRetriesPerRequest: null });

const queues = {
  chainSummarize:    new Queue('chain-summarize',    { connection }),
  stmMigrate:        new Queue('stm-migrate',        { connection }),
  segmentUpdate:     new Queue('segment-update',     { connection }),
  heatCheck:         new Queue('heat-check',         { connection }),
  lpmPromote:        new Queue('lpm-promote',        { connection }),
  mtmEvict:          new Queue('mtm-evict',          { connection }),
};
```

Each queue gets default options: `attempts: 3`, exponential backoff, `removeOnComplete: 1000`, `removeOnFail: 5000`.

## 1.4 Workers

Spawn one BullMQ `Worker` per queue, each pointed at its handler. Workers can run in the same Bun process or a separate one — your call. Concurrency depends on LLM rate limits (start with `concurrency: 5`).

## 1.5 Hono Routes

```
POST /users                  → create user, init profile rows
POST /chat                   → the main pipeline
GET  /memory/:user_id        → debug inspector
```

Now you're live. Move to per-request flow.

---

# PART 2: Per-Message Request Flow (Synchronous Path)

This is what happens **inside** a single `POST /chat` request. Goal: minimize latency. Everything LLM-heavy except the final response is **deferred to background jobs**.

```
POST /chat
Body: { user_id: "u123", message: "Should I do more cardio?" }
```

## 2.1 Receive Request, Validate

- Validate `user_id` exists in `users` table.
- Generate `query_id` (UUID), `timestamp = Date.now()`.

## 2.2 Embed the Query

One synchronous call to your embedding model:
```
query_embedding = embed("Should I do more cardio?")  // 1536-d vector
```

This is the only embedding call on the hot path. ~50–200ms typically.

## 2.3 STM Retrieval (SQLite Only)

```sql
SELECT page_id, query, response, timestamp, meta_chain, chain_id
FROM stm_pages
WHERE user_id = 'u123'
ORDER BY timestamp ASC
LIMIT 7;
```

Returns all current STM pages (up to 7). No filtering, no scoring — STM is small enough to use whole.

## 2.4 MTM Retrieval (Two-Stage: Qdrant → SQLite → Qdrant)

### Stage 1: Find top-m segments (m=5)

**Qdrant query** on `mtm_segments`:
```ts
qdrant.search('mtm_segments', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } }] },
  limit: 10,   // pull a few extra; we'll re-rank with Jaccard
  with_payload: true,
});
```

Returns top 10 candidate `segment_id`s with cosine scores and keyword payloads.

### Stage 1.5: Re-rank with F_score (add Jaccard)

For each candidate:
```ts
// Extract keywords from query (LLM call OR lightweight local extractor)
// Optimization: skip LLM here, do simple keyword extraction (regex / stopword filter)
// Reason: this is hot path, can't afford LLM call
const query_keywords = extractKeywordsLocally(message);

for (candidate of candidates) {
  const jaccard = computeJaccard(candidate.payload.keywords, query_keywords);
  candidate.f_score = candidate.cosine + jaccard;
}

const top_m = candidates.sort((a,b) => b.f_score - a.f_score).slice(0, 5);
```

> **Important call-out**: the paper uses LLM-extracted keywords. On the hot path you can't afford that. Two options:
> 1. **Hot-path local keyword extraction** (regex + stopwords) — fast, "good enough"
> 2. **Pre-cache** query-side keyword extraction in a tiny LRU
>
> Use option 1 for v1.

### Stage 2: Find top-k pages within those segments (k=10)

**Qdrant query** on `mtm_pages`:
```ts
qdrant.search('mtm_pages', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: {
    must: [
      { key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } },
      { key: 'segment_id', match: { any: top_m.map(s => s.id) } },
    ],
  },
  limit: 10,
});
```

### Stage 2.5: Hydrate pages from SQLite

Qdrant returns IDs; full text comes from SQLite:
```sql
SELECT page_id, query, response, timestamp, meta_chain
FROM mtm_pages
WHERE page_id IN (?, ?, ?, ...);
```

### Stage 2.6: Update segment access stats (deferred)

You **don't** want to write to SQLite synchronously here — too many small writes per request. Instead, **enqueue** a lightweight job (or batch in memory and flush periodically) that increments `n_visit` and updates `last_access_time` for the 5 retrieved segments. Or just do it inline with a single batched `UPDATE` — it's fast in SQLite. Pick one; either works.

## 2.5 LPM Retrieval (Mixed)

Four parallel fetches:

### a) Static profiles (SQLite)
```sql
SELECT * FROM user_profile WHERE user_id = 'u123';
SELECT * FROM agent_profile WHERE user_id = 'u123';
```

### b) User Traits — all 90 (SQLite)
```sql
SELECT traits FROM user_traits WHERE user_id = 'u123';
```

### c) User KB top-10 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
```ts
qdrant.search('lpm_user_kb', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } }] },
  limit: 10,
});
// then hydrate facts from SQLite by entry_id
```

### d) Agent Traits top-10 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
Same pattern.

All four can run in parallel via `Promise.all`.

## 2.6 Build the Final Prompt

Stitch everything together:

```
[System prompt]
You are a personal AI assistant. Use the memory below.

[User Profile]      ← LPM static
[Agent Profile]     ← LPM static
[User Traits]       ← LPM 90 dims
[User KB top-10]    ← LPM dynamic facts
[Agent Traits top-10] ← LPM dynamic
[MTM Pages top-10]  ← retrieved Q&As from past topics
[STM Pages]         ← last 7 Q&As
[Current Query]: "Should I do more cardio?"
```

Trim for token budget if needed.

## 2.7 Generate Response (LLM Call)

One LLM call. This is the bulk of request latency (~1–3s).

## 2.8 Persist the New Page to STM

This is the **only mandatory write** on the hot path:

```sql
BEGIN;
INSERT INTO stm_pages (page_id, user_id, query, response, timestamp, chain_id)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL);  -- chain_id filled by background job

-- Check if STM is over capacity
SELECT page_id FROM stm_pages WHERE user_id = ?
  ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 1;
-- If count > 7, this is the page to evict
COMMIT;
```

Wrap in a SQLite transaction. `meta_chain` and `chain_id` left null — they'll be filled by the `chain-summarize` job.

## 2.9 Return Response to User

```
HTTP 200 { response: "...", page_id: "..." }
```

**Total user-facing latency**: embed (200ms) + LLM (2s) + DB ops (<50ms) ≈ **2.3s**. Everything else happens after this point.

## 2.10 Enqueue Background Jobs

Right before (or right after) returning the response:

```ts
// Always
await queues.chainSummarize.add('summarize', { page_id, user_id });

// If STM count > 7
if (stm_count > 7) {
  await queues.stmMigrate.add('migrate', { 
    page_id: oldest_page_id, 
    user_id 
  });
}
```

Done. Response is out, user is happy. The real work begins now.

---

# PART 3: Background Jobs (Async, Chained)

This is the heart of MemoryOS. Each job has a single responsibility and chains into the next.

## 3.1 `chain-summarize` Job

**Triggered by**: every new STM page.

**Steps**:

1. **Load** the new page and the last few STM pages (e.g., previous 3) for context.
2. **LLM call #1 — chain linkage**: ask the LLM "Is this new page topically continuous with the previous chain (chain_id=X)?"
   - YES → set `chain_id = X` on the new page
   - NO → set `chain_id = new_uuid()` (start a new chain)
3. **LLM call #2 — summarization**: ask the LLM to summarize all pages in the current chain into one paragraph.
4. **Write back**:
   ```sql
   UPDATE stm_pages SET chain_id = ?, meta_chain = ? WHERE page_id = ?;
   ```

**Cost**: 2 LLM calls per user message. Optimizable: combine into one call ("decide linkage AND summarize") for cost savings.

**Failure mode**: if it fails after retries, the page stays in STM without a chain — degraded but functional. Retrieval still works.

## 3.2 `stm-migrate` Job (STM → MTM)

**Triggered by**: STM has >7 pages.

**Steps**:

1. **Load oldest STM page** (the one chosen for eviction).
2. **Embed it** — concatenate `(query + response)` → embedding vector.
3. **LLM-extract keywords** from the page (this can run in parallel with embedding to save time).
4. **Find candidate segments** in MTM via Qdrant search (filter by `user_id`, limit 10).
5. **Compute F_score** for each candidate:
   - cosine (from Qdrant)
   - Jaccard (between page keywords and segment keywords from SQLite)
   - sum = F_score
6. **Decision**:
   - If max F_score > 0.6 → assign page to that segment
   - Else → **create a new segment** (LLM call to generate initial summary from this single page)
7. **Write to SQLite** (transaction):
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO mtm_pages (...) VALUES (...);
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET 
     l_interaction = l_interaction + 1,
     last_access_time = ?
   WHERE segment_id = ?;
   DELETE FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id = ?;
   ```
8. **Write to Qdrant**:
   ```ts
   qdrant.upsert('mtm_pages', { id, vector, payload });
   // If new segment: qdrant.upsert('mtm_segments', { ... })
   ```
9. **Chain next jobs**:
   ```ts
   await queues.segmentUpdate.add('update', { segment_id });
   await queues.heatCheck.add('check', { segment_id });
   ```

**Cost**: 1 embedding + 1–2 LLM calls (keyword extract + possibly new segment summary).

**Failure mode**: page is in flight between STM and MTM. Idempotency matters — use unique `page_id` so retries don't double-insert. If the job fails after deleting from STM but before inserting into MTM, the data lives in the **job payload itself** — retries replay from there. Better: do the MTM insert BEFORE deleting from STM.

## 3.3 `segment-update` Job

**Triggered by**: a new page joined an existing segment.

**Steps**:

1. **Load segment** + **all its pages** from SQLite.
2. **LLM call** to regenerate the segment summary based on all pages.
3. **LLM call** (or merge logic) to update the segment's keyword set.
4. **Re-embed** the new summary.
5. **Write back**:
   ```sql
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET summary = ?, keywords = ? WHERE segment_id = ?;
   ```
   ```ts
   qdrant.upsert('mtm_segments', { id: segment_id, vector: new_emb, payload: { keywords } });
   ```

**Optimization**: don't regenerate on every single new page. Batch — only re-summarize every N pages or when segment grows by 20%. Otherwise this is very LLM-call-heavy.

**Failure mode**: stale summary. Fine — retrieval still works on old summary, just slightly less accurate.

## 3.4 `heat-check` Job

**Triggered by**: a segment was just modified.

**Steps**:

1. **Load segment** metadata from SQLite:
   ```sql
   SELECT n_visit, l_interaction, last_access_time FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id = ?;
   ```
2. **Compute heat** in-app:
   ```ts
   const dt = (Date.now() - last_access_time) / 1000;
   const r_recency = Math.exp(-dt / 1e7);
   const heat = n_visit + l_interaction + r_recency;
   ```
3. **Branches**:
   - If `heat > 5` → enqueue `lpm-promote` for this segment.
   - Count total segments for this user. If `> 200` → enqueue `mtm-evict`.

**Cost**: ~zero. Just SQLite reads + a queue write.

**Note**: you don't need to store heat. It's always computed from inputs. R_recency is naturally fresh.

## 3.5 `lpm-promote` Job

**Triggered by**: segment heat > 5.

**Steps**:

1. **Load segment** + all its pages.
2. **LLM call(s)** to extract:
   - **Facts about the user** → candidates for `user_kb`
   - **Facts about the agent's behavior** → candidates for `agent_traits`
   - **Trait scores** for the 90 dimensions → patch `user_traits`
3. **Write to LPM** (SQLite first, then Qdrant):
   ```sql
   -- User KB (FIFO 100)
   INSERT INTO user_kb (entry_id, user_id, fact, created_at) VALUES (...);
   DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE user_id = ? AND entry_id NOT IN (
     SELECT entry_id FROM user_kb WHERE user_id = ?
     ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100
   );
   -- Same pattern for agent_traits
   -- For user_traits: merge into the JSON blob
   UPDATE user_traits SET traits = ? WHERE user_id = ?;
   ```
4. **Embed each new KB / agent_trait entry** and upsert into Qdrant:
   ```ts
   qdrant.upsert('lpm_user_kb', { id, vector, payload: { user_id, entry_id } });
   qdrant.upsert('lpm_agent_traits', { id, vector, payload: { user_id, entry_id } });
   ```
   Also delete vectors for FIFO-evicted entries.
5. **Reset segment's `l_interaction`**:
   ```sql
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id = ?;
   ```
   This is the critical "cooldown" step — otherwise segments would keep re-promoting.

**Cost**: 1–3 LLM calls + several embeddings. Heaviest job in the system, but rare (only triggered when heat crosses threshold).

**Failure mode**: partial promotion. Idempotency: deduplicate by `(user_id, fact)` text hash before insert. If the job ran once partially, the dedup makes a retry safe.

## 3.6 `mtm-evict` Job

**Triggered by**: MTM segment count > 200 for a user.

**Steps**:

1. **Find lowest-heat segment**:
   ```sql
   SELECT segment_id,
     n_visit + l_interaction + exp((last_access_time - ?) / 1e7) AS heat
   FROM mtm_segments
   WHERE user_id = ?
   ORDER BY heat ASC
   LIMIT 1;
   ```
2. **Delete from SQLite** (cascade pages):
   ```sql
   BEGIN;
   DELETE FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id = ?;
   DELETE FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id = ?;
   COMMIT;
   ```
3. **Delete from Qdrant**:
   ```ts
   qdrant.delete('mtm_segments', { points: [segment_id] });
   qdrant.delete('mtm_pages', { filter: { must: [{ key: 'segment_id', match: { value: segment_id } }] } });
   ```

**Cost**: tiny. No LLM calls.

**Note**: the facts already in LPM survive — the eviction only removes the working-zone copy.

---

# PART 4: Job Chain Visualization

```
User message arrives
        │
        ▼
[Synchronous: respond to user] ────► Response sent
        │
        ▼
queues.chainSummarize ────► updates STM page meta_chain
        │
   (independently, if STM > 7)
        ▼
queues.stmMigrate
   ├─ Embed + extract keywords (LLM)
   ├─ Find/create MTM segment
   ├─ Write to SQLite + Qdrant
   └─► queues.segmentUpdate ──► refresh segment summary
       │
       └─► queues.heatCheck ──► compute heat
              │
              ├─► if heat > 5: queues.lpmPromote ──► write LPM, reset l_interaction
              │
              └─► if MTM > 200: queues.mtmEvict ──► drop coldest
```

---

# PART 5: Edge Cases and Cross-Cutting Concerns

## 5.1 Cold-Start (Brand-New User, First Message)

- `users` row created on first call → `POST /users` or implicit on `/chat`.
- `user_profile`, `agent_profile`, `user_traits` initialized with defaults.
- `stm_pages`, `mtm_*`, `lpm_*` all empty.
- MTM retrieval returns nothing → that's fine, the LLM still has STM (which has 1 page after this) and a blank persona to work with.
- Response generation works on default prompt + just the current query.
- After 7 messages, STM starts overflowing → MTM begins populating. After repeated topics → LPM kicks in.

## 5.2 Idempotency

All jobs must be idempotent. Use these patterns:
- **Deterministic IDs**: derive `page_id` from `(user_id, timestamp, hash(query))`. Retries reinsert the same row → noop.
- **`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`** on SQLite inserts.
- **Qdrant `upsert`** is already idempotent.
- **Job dedup**: use BullMQ's `jobId` option to prevent duplicate enqueues.

## 5.3 Transaction Boundaries

SQLite + Qdrant can't be transactional together. Rule of thumb:

- **Write to SQLite first** (in a transaction). SQLite is source of truth.
- **Then write to Qdrant**. If this fails, the job will retry — SQLite still has the data.
- **Never** delete from SQLite before Qdrant confirms — orphaned vectors are worse than orphaned rows.

For complex jobs, structure as: SQLite write → Qdrant write → SQLite "committed" flag. On retry, check the flag.

## 5.4 Multi-User Isolation

- Every SQLite query has `WHERE user_id = ?`.
- Every Qdrant query has `filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', ... }] }`.
- Every job payload includes `user_id`.
- Centralize this in helper functions so you can't forget. One leak and users see each other's memory.

## 5.5 Hot Path vs Async — Why It Matters

If you did all this synchronously, a single user message would take **10–30 seconds** (multiple LLM calls). The split above gives ~2.3s user-facing latency by deferring:
- Chain summarization
- STM → MTM migration
- Segment re-summarization
- Heat checks
- LPM promotion
- Eviction

The user gets a fast response. Memory state catches up within a few seconds.

## 5.6 Embedding Model Consistency

Use the **same embedding model everywhere**. If you ever migrate models, you must re-embed all vectors. Pin the model + dim in a config and never change without a migration plan.

## 5.7 Concurrency Within a User

If a user sends two messages quickly, you can have **two `stm-migrate` jobs** in flight for the same user. Both might try to create a new segment for similar pages. Mitigations:
- Use BullMQ's `groupId` (Pro) or implement a per-user mutex via Redis (`SETNX` with TTL).
- Or accept it — minor duplication of segments self-corrects later as segment-update merges them.

For v1: per-user mutex on writes is the cleanest.

## 5.8 What Stays Synchronous

Be explicit about it:
- Query embedding ✅ sync (need it for retrieval)
- All retrieval (STM, MTM, LPM) ✅ sync
- Response LLM call ✅ sync
- Writing new STM page ✅ sync
- Everything else ❌ async

## 5.9 Observability

For each background job, log: `job_id`, `user_id`, `duration`, `LLM_tokens`. BullMQ has built-in metrics. Add a debug endpoint that dumps a user's STM/MTM/LPM state — invaluable for debugging.

## 5.10 Heat Computation

Never store heat. Compute it via a SQL expression:
```sql
n_visit + l_interaction + exp(CAST(last_access_time - strftime('%s','now')*1000 AS REAL) / 1e10) AS heat
```
(adjust units — paper uses seconds, μ=1e7 sec ≈ 115 days.)

This means R_recency is always fresh, no cron needed.

---

# Summary: The Pipeline in One Page

```
TIME = T0:  User POSTs message
TIME = T0 + 50ms:   embed(query)
TIME = T0 + 80ms:   STM read (SQLite)
TIME = T0 + 150ms:  MTM stage 1 (Qdrant) + stage 2 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
TIME = T0 + 200ms:  LPM read (SQLite + Qdrant in parallel)
TIME = T0 + 220ms:  build final prompt
TIME = T0 + 2.2s:   LLM response
TIME = T0 + 2.25s:  write new STM page (SQLite)
TIME = T0 + 2.3s:   enqueue background jobs → RETURN to user

[BACKGROUND, async after response]
TIME = T0 + 2.5s:   chain-summarize finishes
TIME = T0 + 3.5s:   stm-migrate finishes (if STM was full)
                    → triggers segment-update + heat-check
TIME = T0 + 5s:     segment-update finishes
TIME = T0 + 5.1s:   heat-check decides: promote / evict / nothing
TIME = T0 + 8s:     lpm-promote finishes (if triggered)
```

Memory state is fully consistent within ~5–10 seconds of the user's message, and the user never waits for any of it.

That's the entire end-to-end pipeline. Want me to flesh out any specific job's code, or draft the Hono `/chat` handler?

```md
# MemoryOS Pipeline on Bun + Hono + Qdrant + SQLite + BullMQ

End-to-end implementation breakdown. Auth: simple email + password.

---

# PART 1: Startup / Initialization (One-Time)

When your Bun app boots, you initialize all four systems before serving traffic.

## 1.1 SQLite Schema

Open a single Bun SQLite file (e.g., `memoryos.db`). Run migrations to create:

\```sql
-- Users
CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- STM
CREATE TABLE stm_pages (
  page_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  query TEXT NOT NULL,
  response TEXT NOT NULL,
  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
  chain_id TEXT,          -- groups pages into a chain
  meta_chain TEXT,        -- LLM summary of the chain (filled async)
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_stm_user_time ON stm_pages(user_id, timestamp);

-- MTM Segments
CREATE TABLE mtm_segments (
  segment_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  summary TEXT NOT NULL,
  keywords TEXT NOT NULL,           -- JSON array
  n_visit INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  l_interaction INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  last_access_time INTEGER NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_mtm_user ON mtm_segments(user_id);

-- MTM Pages
CREATE TABLE mtm_pages (
  page_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  segment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  query TEXT NOT NULL,
  response TEXT NOT NULL,
  timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
  meta_chain TEXT,
  keywords TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (segment_id) REFERENCES mtm_segments(segment_id)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_mtm_pages_segment ON mtm_pages(segment_id);

-- LPM static profiles
CREATE TABLE user_profile (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT, gender TEXT, birth_year INTEGER,
  extras TEXT  -- JSON
);
CREATE TABLE agent_profile (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  role TEXT, character TEXT,
  extras TEXT
);

-- LPM dynamic — User Traits (90 dims)
CREATE TABLE user_traits (
  user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  traits TEXT NOT NULL  -- JSON: {"fitness_motivation": 0.9, ...}
);

-- LPM dynamic — User KB (FIFO 100)
CREATE TABLE user_kb (
  entry_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  fact TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_kb_user_time ON user_kb(user_id, created_at);

-- LPM dynamic — Agent Traits (FIFO 100)
CREATE TABLE agent_traits (
  entry_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  trait TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_traits_user_time ON agent_traits(user_id, created_at);
\```

## 1.2 Qdrant Collections

Create four collections (vector_dim depends on your embedder, e.g., 1536 for OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small`).

\```
mtm_segments
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)

mtm_pages
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload indexes: user_id (keyword), segment_id (keyword)

lpm_user_kb
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)

lpm_agent_traits
  vector: { size: 1536, distance: Cosine }
  payload index: user_id (keyword)
\```

No collection for STM (only 7 pages, retrieved as a whole).

## 1.3 ioredis + BullMQ Setup

Spin up one Redis connection. Define queues:

\```ts
const connection = new IORedis({ maxRetriesPerRequest: null });

const queues = {
  chainSummarize:    new Queue('chain-summarize',    { connection }),
  stmMigrate:        new Queue('stm-migrate',        { connection }),
  segmentUpdate:     new Queue('segment-update',     { connection }),
  heatCheck:         new Queue('heat-check',         { connection }),
  lpmPromote:        new Queue('lpm-promote',        { connection }),
  mtmEvict:          new Queue('mtm-evict',          { connection }),
};
\```

Each queue gets default options: `attempts: 3`, exponential backoff, `removeOnComplete: 1000`, `removeOnFail: 5000`.

## 1.4 Workers

Spawn one BullMQ `Worker` per queue, each pointed at its handler. Workers can run in the same Bun process or a separate one — your call. Concurrency depends on LLM rate limits (start with `concurrency: 5`).

## 1.5 Hono Routes

\```
POST /auth/signup            → create user (email + password), init profile rows
POST /auth/login             → return JWT/session
POST /chat                   → the main pipeline (auth required)
GET  /memory/:user_id        → debug inspector (auth required)
\```

Now you're live. Move to per-request flow.

---

# PART 2: Per-Message Request Flow (Synchronous Path)

This is what happens **inside** a single `POST /chat` request. Goal: minimize latency. Everything LLM-heavy except the final response is **deferred to background jobs**.

\```
POST /chat
Headers: Authorization: Bearer <token>
Body: { message: "Should I do more cardio?" }
\```

## 2.1 Receive Request, Validate

- Validate auth token → resolve `user_id`.
- Validate `user_id` exists in `users` table.
- Generate `query_id` (UUID), `timestamp = Date.now()`.

## 2.2 Embed the Query

One synchronous call to your embedding model:
\```
query_embedding = embed("Should I do more cardio?")  // 1536-d vector
\```

This is the only embedding call on the hot path. ~50–200ms typically.

## 2.3 STM Retrieval (SQLite Only)

\```sql
SELECT page_id, query, response, timestamp, meta_chain, chain_id
FROM stm_pages
WHERE user_id = 'u123'
ORDER BY timestamp ASC
LIMIT 7;
\```

Returns all current STM pages (up to 7). No filtering, no scoring — STM is small enough to use whole.

## 2.4 MTM Retrieval (Two-Stage: Qdrant → SQLite → Qdrant)

### Stage 1: Find top-m segments (m=5)

**Qdrant query** on `mtm_segments`:
\```ts
qdrant.search('mtm_segments', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } }] },
  limit: 10,   // pull a few extra; we'll re-rank with Jaccard
  with_payload: true,
});
\```

Returns top 10 candidate `segment_id`s with cosine scores and keyword payloads.

### Stage 1.5: Re-rank with F_score (add Jaccard)

For each candidate:
\```ts
// Extract keywords from query (LLM call OR lightweight local extractor)
// Optimization: skip LLM here, do simple keyword extraction (regex / stopword filter)
const query_keywords = extractKeywordsLocally(message);

for (candidate of candidates) {
  const jaccard = computeJaccard(candidate.payload.keywords, query_keywords);
  candidate.f_score = candidate.cosine + jaccard;
}

const top_m = candidates.sort((a,b) => b.f_score - a.f_score).slice(0, 5);
\```

> **Important**: the paper uses LLM-extracted keywords. On the hot path you can't afford that. Use local keyword extraction (regex + stopwords) for v1.

### Stage 2: Find top-k pages within those segments (k=10)

\```ts
qdrant.search('mtm_pages', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: {
    must: [
      { key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } },
      { key: 'segment_id', match: { any: top_m.map(s => s.id) } },
    ],
  },
  limit: 10,
});
\```

### Stage 2.5: Hydrate pages from SQLite

\```sql
SELECT page_id, query, response, timestamp, meta_chain
FROM mtm_pages
WHERE page_id IN (?, ?, ?, ...);
\```

### Stage 2.6: Update segment access stats (deferred)

Batch a single UPDATE for the 5 retrieved segments to increment `n_visit` and refresh `last_access_time`. Cheap in SQLite — keep inline.

## 2.5 LPM Retrieval (Mixed)

Four parallel fetches:

### a) Static profiles (SQLite)
\```sql
SELECT * FROM user_profile WHERE user_id = 'u123';
SELECT * FROM agent_profile WHERE user_id = 'u123';
\```

### b) User Traits — all 90 (SQLite)
\```sql
SELECT traits FROM user_traits WHERE user_id = 'u123';
\```

### c) User KB top-10 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
\```ts
qdrant.search('lpm_user_kb', {
  vector: query_embedding,
  filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', match: { value: 'u123' } }] },
  limit: 10,
});
// then hydrate facts from SQLite by entry_id
\```

### d) Agent Traits top-10 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
Same pattern.

All four can run in parallel via `Promise.all`.

## 2.6 Build the Final Prompt

\```
[System prompt]
You are a personal AI assistant. Use the memory below.

[User Profile]      ← LPM static
[Agent Profile]     ← LPM static
[User Traits]       ← LPM 90 dims
[User KB top-10]    ← LPM dynamic facts
[Agent Traits top-10] ← LPM dynamic
[MTM Pages top-10]  ← retrieved Q&As from past topics
[STM Pages]         ← last 7 Q&As
[Current Query]: "Should I do more cardio?"
\```

Trim for token budget if needed.

## 2.7 Generate Response (LLM Call)

One LLM call. This is the bulk of request latency (~1–3s).

## 2.8 Persist the New Page to STM

\```sql
BEGIN;
INSERT INTO stm_pages (page_id, user_id, query, response, timestamp, chain_id)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL);  -- chain_id filled by background job

-- Check if STM is over capacity
SELECT page_id FROM stm_pages WHERE user_id = ?
  ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 1;
COMMIT;
\```

`meta_chain` and `chain_id` left null — they'll be filled by the `chain-summarize` job.

## 2.9 Return Response to User

\```
HTTP 200 { response: "...", page_id: "..." }
\```

**Total user-facing latency**: embed (200ms) + LLM (2s) + DB ops (<50ms) ≈ **2.3s**.

## 2.10 Enqueue Background Jobs

\```ts
await queues.chainSummarize.add('summarize', { page_id, user_id });

if (stm_count > 7) {
  await queues.stmMigrate.add('migrate', { 
    page_id: oldest_page_id, 
    user_id 
  });
}
\```

---

# PART 3: Background Jobs (Async, Chained)

## 3.1 `chain-summarize` Job

**Triggered by**: every new STM page.

**Steps**:
1. Load the new page and the last few STM pages (e.g., previous 3) for context.
2. **LLM call #1 — chain linkage**: "Is this new page topically continuous with the previous chain (chain_id=X)?"
   - YES → set `chain_id = X` on the new page
   - NO → set `chain_id = new_uuid()` (start a new chain)
3. **LLM call #2 — summarization**: summarize all pages in the current chain into one paragraph.
4. **Write back**:
   \```sql
   UPDATE stm_pages SET chain_id = ?, meta_chain = ? WHERE page_id = ?;
   \```

**Cost**: 2 LLM calls per user message. Optimizable: combine into one call.

## 3.2 `stm-migrate` Job (STM → MTM)

**Triggered by**: STM has >7 pages.

**Steps**:
1. **Load oldest STM page**.
2. **Embed it** — concatenate `(query + response)` → embedding vector.
3. **LLM-extract keywords** from the page.
4. **Find candidate segments** in MTM via Qdrant search (filter by `user_id`, limit 10).
5. **Compute F_score** for each candidate (cosine + Jaccard).
6. **Decision**:
   - If max F_score > 0.6 → assign page to that segment
   - Else → create a new segment (LLM call to generate initial summary)
7. **Write to SQLite** (transaction):
   \```sql
   INSERT INTO mtm_pages (...) VALUES (...);
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET 
     l_interaction = l_interaction + 1,
     last_access_time = ?
   WHERE segment_id = ?;
   DELETE FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id = ?;
   \```
8. **Write to Qdrant**:
   \```ts
   qdrant.upsert('mtm_pages', { id, vector, payload });
   // If new segment: qdrant.upsert('mtm_segments', { ... })
   \```
9. **Chain next jobs**:
   \```ts
   await queues.segmentUpdate.add('update', { segment_id });
   await queues.heatCheck.add('check', { segment_id });
   \```

## 3.3 `segment-update` Job

**Triggered by**: a new page joined an existing segment.

**Steps**:
1. Load segment + all its pages from SQLite.
2. LLM call to regenerate the segment summary based on all pages.
3. LLM call (or merge logic) to update the segment's keyword set.
4. Re-embed the new summary.
5. Write back:
   \```sql
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET summary = ?, keywords = ? WHERE segment_id = ?;
   \```
   \```ts
   qdrant.upsert('mtm_segments', { id: segment_id, vector: new_emb, payload: { keywords } });
   \```

**Optimization**: batch — only re-summarize every N pages or when segment grows by 20%.

## 3.4 `heat-check` Job

**Triggered by**: a segment was just modified.

**Steps**:
1. Load segment metadata from SQLite.
2. Compute heat in-app:
   \```ts
   const dt = (Date.now() - last_access_time) / 1000;
   const r_recency = Math.exp(-dt / 1e7);
   const heat = n_visit + l_interaction + r_recency;
   \```
3. Branches:
   - If `heat > 5` → enqueue `lpm-promote` for this segment.
   - Count total segments for this user. If `> 200` → enqueue `mtm-evict`.

## 3.5 `lpm-promote` Job

**Triggered by**: segment heat > 5.

**Steps**:
1. Load segment + all its pages.
2. LLM call(s) to extract:
   - Facts about the user → candidates for `user_kb`
   - Facts about the agent's behavior → candidates for `agent_traits`
   - Trait scores for the 90 dimensions → patch `user_traits`
3. Write to LPM (SQLite first, then Qdrant):
   \```sql
   INSERT INTO user_kb (entry_id, user_id, fact, created_at) VALUES (...);
   DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE user_id = ? AND entry_id NOT IN (
     SELECT entry_id FROM user_kb WHERE user_id = ?
     ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100
   );
   UPDATE user_traits SET traits = ? WHERE user_id = ?;
   \```
4. Embed each new KB / agent_trait entry and upsert into Qdrant.
5. Reset segment's `l_interaction`:
   \```sql
   UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id = ?;
   \```

## 3.6 `mtm-evict` Job

**Triggered by**: MTM segment count > 200 for a user.

**Steps**:
1. Find lowest-heat segment via SQL:
   \```sql
   SELECT segment_id,
     n_visit + l_interaction + exp((last_access_time - ?) / 1e7) AS heat
   FROM mtm_segments
   WHERE user_id = ?
   ORDER BY heat ASC
   LIMIT 1;
   \```
2. Delete from SQLite (cascade pages):
   \```sql
   BEGIN;
   DELETE FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id = ?;
   DELETE FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id = ?;
   COMMIT;
   \```
3. Delete from Qdrant.

---

# PART 4: Job Chain Visualization

\```
User message arrives
        │
        ▼
[Synchronous: respond to user] ────► Response sent
        │
        ▼
queues.chainSummarize ────► updates STM page meta_chain
        │
   (independently, if STM > 7)
        ▼
queues.stmMigrate
   ├─ Embed + extract keywords (LLM)
   ├─ Find/create MTM segment
   ├─ Write to SQLite + Qdrant
   └─► queues.segmentUpdate ──► refresh segment summary
       │
       └─► queues.heatCheck ──► compute heat
              │
              ├─► if heat > 5: queues.lpmPromote ──► write LPM, reset l_interaction
              │
              └─► if MTM > 200: queues.mtmEvict ──► drop coldest
\```

---

# PART 5: Edge Cases and Cross-Cutting Concerns

## 5.1 Cold-Start (Brand-New User, First Message)
- `users` row created on signup.
- `user_profile`, `agent_profile`, `user_traits` initialized with defaults.
- All memory tables empty.
- MTM retrieval returns nothing → fine, LLM still gets STM + blank persona.

## 5.2 Idempotency
- Deterministic IDs: derive `page_id` from `(user_id, timestamp, hash(query))`.
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on SQLite inserts.
- Qdrant `upsert` is already idempotent.
- BullMQ `jobId` option to prevent duplicate enqueues.

## 5.3 Transaction Boundaries
- Write to SQLite first (in a transaction). SQLite is source of truth.
- Then write to Qdrant. If this fails, the job will retry.
- Never delete from SQLite before Qdrant confirms.

## 5.4 Multi-User Isolation
- Every SQLite query has `WHERE user_id = ?`.
- Every Qdrant query has `filter: { must: [{ key: 'user_id', ... }] }`.
- Every job payload includes `user_id`.

## 5.5 Hot Path vs Async
~2.3s user-facing latency by deferring all LLM-heavy work to background jobs.

## 5.6 Embedding Model Consistency
Use the same embedding model everywhere. Pin model + dim in config.

## 5.7 Concurrency Within a User
Per-user mutex via Redis `SETNX` with TTL — cleanest for v1.

## 5.8 What Stays Synchronous
- Query embedding ✅
- All retrieval ✅
- Response LLM ✅
- Writing new STM page ✅
- Everything else ❌

## 5.9 Observability
Log per job: `job_id`, `user_id`, `duration`, `LLM_tokens`. Add a `/memory/:user_id` debug endpoint.

## 5.10 Heat Computation
Never store heat. Compute via SQL expression on read.

---

# Summary Timeline

\```
TIME = T0:        User POSTs message
TIME = T0 + 50ms:   embed(query)
TIME = T0 + 80ms:   STM read (SQLite)
TIME = T0 + 150ms:  MTM stage 1 (Qdrant) + stage 2 (Qdrant + SQLite hydrate)
TIME = T0 + 200ms:  LPM read (SQLite + Qdrant in parallel)
TIME = T0 + 220ms:  build final prompt
TIME = T0 + 2.2s:   LLM response
TIME = T0 + 2.25s:  write new STM page (SQLite)
TIME = T0 + 2.3s:   enqueue background jobs → RETURN to user

[BACKGROUND]
TIME = T0 + 2.5s:   chain-summarize finishes
TIME = T0 + 3.5s:   stm-migrate finishes (if STM was full)
TIME = T0 + 5s:     segment-update finishes
TIME = T0 + 5.1s:   heat-check decides: promote / evict / nothing
TIME = T0 + 8s:     lpm-promote finishes (if triggered)
\```

# The Three Memory Loops — End to End

A "loop" here = the **full lifecycle** of memory at that tier: ingress (data coming in), in-place updates (heat, counters, summaries), and egress (data leaving via promotion or eviction). Each loop touches specific DBs in a specific order.

---

# LOOP 1: STM Loop (Short-Term Memory)

**Scope**: A bounded queue of 7 Q&A pages per user. Lives entirely in **Bun SQLite** (no Qdrant, no Redis state). The fastest, simplest loop.

## A. Ingress — New page enters STM

**Trigger**: User sends a chat message and gets a response.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Insert new page (query, response, timestamp) | SQLite | `INSERT INTO stm_pages` |
| 2 | Check current count for this user | SQLite | `SELECT COUNT(*)` |
| 3 | If count > 7, identify oldest page | SQLite | `SELECT ... ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 1` |
| 4 | Enqueue chain-summarize job | Redis (BullMQ) | `queue.add('chain-summarize', {page_id})` |
| 5 | If overflow, enqueue stm-migrate | Redis (BullMQ) | `queue.add('stm-migrate', {page_id})` |

**At this moment**: STM has 7 or 8 entries. The oldest is "in flight" toward MTM.

## B. In-place update — `chain-summarize` runs (async)

**Trigger**: BullMQ worker picks up the `chain-summarize` job.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load new page + last 3 STM pages | SQLite | `SELECT ... ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 4` |
| 2 | LLM call: is new page continuous with prior chain? | (external LLM) | API call |
| 3 | Decide `chain_id`: reuse existing or generate new | (in-app) | UUID logic |
| 4 | LLM call: summarize the chain | (external LLM) | API call |
| 5 | Update the page row | SQLite | `UPDATE stm_pages SET chain_id=?, meta_chain=?` |

**No Qdrant interaction in STM. Ever.** STM is fully retrieved as a list, not searched semantically.

## C. Egress — STM → MTM (handled by `stm-migrate` job — described in Loop 2 below)

The oldest page **leaves STM only when migrated**. The `stm-migrate` worker:
1. Inserts the page into MTM (SQLite + Qdrant).
2. Deletes it from STM (SQLite).

So STM stays bounded at ≤7 within a few seconds of going over.

## D. STM retrieval (read path)

**Trigger**: Every user request.

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM stm_pages WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 7` |

No scoring, no Jaccard, no embeddings. Just dump the queue into the prompt.

## STM loop summary

```
[User msg] → SQLite.INSERT → Redis.queue(chain-summarize, stm-migrate)
                                       │
                                       ├─ worker → LLM → SQLite.UPDATE (meta_chain)
                                       │
                                       └─ worker → kicks off MTM loop
```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (always), Redis (always for queue), LLM API (background).
- **No Qdrant at all.**
- **No heat logic at all.**
- Hardest invariant: STM size ≤ 7 (enforced by checking on each insert).

---

# LOOP 2: MTM Loop (Mid-Term Memory)

**Scope**: Up to 200 segments per user, each containing many pages. This is where **Qdrant and SQLite work together** and where **heat-based logic kicks in**.

## A. Ingress — Page arrives from STM

**Trigger**: `stm-migrate` worker picks up a job.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load the migrating page from SQLite | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id=?` |
| 2 | Embed page content (query + response) | (external embed API) | API call |
| 3 | LLM-extract keywords | (external LLM) | API call |
| 4 | Find candidate segments | **Qdrant** | `search(mtm_segments, vector=query_emb, filter=user_id, limit=10)` |
| 5 | Pull candidate keyword sets for Jaccard | SQLite | `SELECT keywords FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id IN (...)` |
| 6 | Compute F_score for each (cosine + Jaccard) | (in-app) | math |
| 7a | If max F_score > 0.6: assign to that segment | — | branch |
| 7b | Else: create a new segment | — | branch |

### Path 7a — Joining existing segment

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_pages (segment_id, ...)` |
| 2 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = l_interaction + 1, last_access_time = NOW()` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_pages, {id, vector, payload={user_id, segment_id, keywords}})` |
| 4 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id=?` |
| 5 | Redis | `queue.add('segment-update', {segment_id})` |
| 6 | Redis | `queue.add('heat-check', {segment_id})` |

### Path 7b — Creating new segment

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | (LLM) | Generate initial segment summary from the page |
| 2 | (embed API) | Embed the new summary |
| 3 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_segments (segment_id, summary, keywords, n_visit=0, l_interaction=1, last_access_time=NOW())` |
| 4 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_pages (segment_id, ...)` |
| 5 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_segments, {id, vector=summary_emb, payload})` |
| 6 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_pages, {id, vector=page_emb, payload})` |
| 7 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM stm_pages` |
| 8 | Redis | `queue.add('heat-check', {segment_id})` |

## B. In-place updates inside MTM

Three things continuously change while a segment lives in MTM:

### B1. Counter: `l_interaction`

- **What**: Number of pages in segment.
- **When updated**: Every time a new page joins (path 7a or 7b).
- **DB**: SQLite — `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = l_interaction + 1`.

### B2. Counter: `n_visit` + `last_access_time`

- **What**: Retrieval popularity + recency.
- **When updated**: Every time a segment is retrieved during `/chat` (Part 2.4, Stage 1/2 of retrieval).
- **DB**: SQLite — batched UPDATE during the request handler (or end-of-request).
  ```sql
  UPDATE mtm_segments
  SET n_visit = n_visit + 1, last_access_time = ?
  WHERE segment_id IN (...);
  ```

### B3. Summary + keywords (re-summarization)

- **What**: The segment's summary text and keyword set, used for retrieval matching.
- **When updated**: `segment-update` job, triggered when a new page joined.
- **Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Load segment + all pages |
| 2 | (LLM) | Regenerate summary covering all pages |
| 3 | (LLM) | Merge/update keyword set |
| 4 | (embed API) | Re-embed new summary |
| 5 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET summary=?, keywords=?` |
| 6 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_segments, {id, vector=new_emb, payload={keywords}})` |

> **Note**: re-embed = the segment's Qdrant point gets a new vector. Qdrant overwrites cleanly on `upsert`.

## C. Heat lifecycle (the core of the MTM loop)

This is where the "heat" idea actually lives. **Heat is never stored** — it's computed from three inputs that ARE stored.

### Heat inputs (stored in SQLite per segment)

| Field | Source | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `n_visit` | incremented on retrieval | int |
| `l_interaction` | incremented on page-join, reset on promotion | int |
| `last_access_time` | refreshed on retrieval or new page-join | timestamp |

### Heat formula (computed on read)

```
dt = (now - last_access_time) / 1000      // seconds
R_recency = exp(-dt / 1e7)                // 0 to 1
Heat = n_visit + l_interaction + R_recency
```

Either compute in app code, or directly in SQL:

```sql
SELECT segment_id,
  n_visit + l_interaction + exp((last_access_time - ?) / 1e10) AS heat
FROM mtm_segments
WHERE user_id = ?;
```

(Adjust units; ms vs sec.)

### When heat is evaluated — `heat-check` job

Triggered after any `stm-migrate` or `segment-update`.

**Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Load segment counters |
| 2 | (in-app) | Compute heat |
| 3 | SQLite | Count user's total segments |
| 4 | (branch) | If heat > 5 → `queue.add('lpm-promote', ...)` |
| 5 | (branch) | If count > 200 → `queue.add('mtm-evict', ...)` |

No DB writes in this job. It's purely a router.

## D. Egress — Two ways out of MTM

### D1. Promotion (heat > 5) → `lpm-promote` job

This is described in detail in Loop 3 below. The key MTM-side effect:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| End | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id=?` |

**Why reset?** If we didn't, the segment would stay above heat=5 forever and re-promote every single time. Reset drops heat, segment must "earn it again."

The segment itself stays in MTM. Only the **derived persona data** moves to LPM.

### D2. Eviction (MTM > 200 segments) → `mtm-evict` job

**Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Find lowest-heat segment via heat-expression ORDER BY ASC LIMIT 1 |
| 2 | SQLite | `BEGIN; DELETE FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id=?; DELETE FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id=?; COMMIT;` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `delete(mtm_pages, filter={segment_id})` |
| 4 | Qdrant | `delete(mtm_segments, points=[segment_id])` |

Both DBs in sync. The segment is gone.

> Note: anything that already promoted to LPM remains. Eviction only loses the working-zone copy.

## E. MTM retrieval (read path)

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Qdrant | `search(mtm_segments, vector, filter=user_id, limit=10)` |
| 2 | SQLite | `SELECT keywords FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id IN (...)` |
| 3 | (in-app) | Compute Jaccard + F_score, pick top-5 |
| 4 | Qdrant | `search(mtm_pages, vector, filter={user_id, segment_id IN top-5}, limit=10)` |
| 5 | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM mtm_pages WHERE page_id IN (...)` |
| 6 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET n_visit = n_visit + 1, last_access_time = ? WHERE segment_id IN (top-5)` |

Heat inputs get bumped automatically on every retrieval.

## MTM loop summary

```
[from STM] ──► stm-migrate worker
                  │
                  ├─ embed page (API)
                  ├─ Qdrant search candidates
                  ├─ SQLite load keywords
                  ├─ F_score → assign or create segment
                  ├─ SQLite insert page + update segment counters
                  ├─ Qdrant upsert page (+ segment if new)
                  ├─ SQLite delete from STM
                  └─► segment-update + heat-check
                          │
                          ├─ segment-update: LLM resummarize → SQLite + Qdrant update
                          └─ heat-check: compute heat
                                ├─ heat > 5 → lpm-promote
                                └─ count > 200 → mtm-evict
```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (heavy), Qdrant (heavy), Redis queue (always), LLM/embed API (background only).
- **Heat fields updated**: on retrieval (n_visit, last_access_time) and on page-join (l_interaction).
- **Heat reset**: only on LPM promotion (l_interaction → 0).

---

# LOOP 3: LPM Loop (Long-term Persona Memory)

**Scope**: Per-user persistent persona. 5 sub-stores. Mix of static (no churn) and dynamic (constantly evolving). The slowest loop — only fires when an MTM segment is hot enough.

## LPM has 5 stores; treat each separately

| Store | DB | Capacity | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user_profile` | SQLite | 1 row | Static |
| `agent_profile` | SQLite | 1 row | Static |
| `user_traits` | SQLite | 1 row (JSON of 90 floats) | Dynamic (merged) |
| `user_kb` | SQLite + Qdrant | FIFO 100 entries | Dynamic (FIFO eviction) |
| `agent_traits` | SQLite + Qdrant | FIFO 100 entries | Dynamic (FIFO eviction) |

## A. Ingress — `lpm-promote` job runs

**Trigger**: `heat-check` decided a segment's heat > 5.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load segment + all pages | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id=?` |
| 2 | LLM call: extract user facts | (LLM) | API call |
| 3 | LLM call: extract agent behavior facts | (LLM) | API call |
| 4 | LLM call: produce trait deltas for 90 dims | (LLM) | API call |

(Can be combined into 1–2 calls to save cost.)

### Writes for User KB

For each new user fact:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 5a | SQLite | `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_kb (entry_id, user_id, fact, created_at)` (dedup by hash) |
| 5b | (embed API) | Embed the fact |
| 5c | Qdrant | `upsert(lpm_user_kb, {id=entry_id, vector, payload={user_id}})` |

Then FIFO eviction:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 6a | SQLite | Find entries beyond the 100 newest |
| 6b | SQLite | `DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE entry_id IN (...)` |
| 6c | Qdrant | `delete(lpm_user_kb, points=[evicted_ids])` |

### Writes for Agent Traits

Same pattern as User KB — SQLite insert + Qdrant upsert + FIFO trim in both.

### Writes for User Traits

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 7a | SQLite | `SELECT traits FROM user_traits WHERE user_id=?` → parse JSON |
| 7b | (in-app) | Merge LLM-produced deltas into the 90-dim object |
| 7c | SQLite | `UPDATE user_traits SET traits=? WHERE user_id=?` |

User Traits is **not** stored in Qdrant — there's no top-10 search on it. The full 90-dim JSON is loaded on every request.

### Final step — reset segment heat

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 8 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id=?` |

This **closes the LPM-promotion loop** by cooling the source segment.

## B. In-place updates inside LPM

**User Profile / Agent Profile (static)**: never updated by background loops. Only via explicit user/admin action.

**User Traits**: updated only during `lpm-promote` jobs (Step 7 above).

**User KB / Agent Traits**: appended during promotion. FIFO trim happens within the same job.

## C. Egress — FIFO eviction

When `user_kb` or `agent_traits` exceeds 100 entries:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Get IDs of entries beyond the newest 100 |
| 2 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE entry_id NOT IN (SELECT entry_id FROM user_kb WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100)` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `delete(lpm_user_kb, points=[evicted_ids])` |

No heat involved. Pure FIFO by `created_at`.

## D. LPM retrieval (read path)

Run in parallel during the request:

| Sub-store | Strategy | DBs |
|---|---|---|
| `user_profile` | Full row | SQLite |
| `agent_profile` | Full row | SQLite |
| `user_traits` | Full 90-dim JSON | SQLite |
| `user_kb` | Top-10 semantic | Qdrant search → SQLite hydrate |
| `agent_traits` | Top-10 semantic | Qdrant search → SQLite hydrate |

All wrapped in a `Promise.all`.

## LPM loop summary

```
[from MTM heat-check] ──► lpm-promote worker
                            │
                            ├─ LLM extract: user facts, agent traits, trait deltas
                            │
                            ├─ user_kb:
                            │     SQLite INSERT
                            │     embed + Qdrant upsert
                            │     SQLite FIFO trim → Qdrant delete
                            │
                            ├─ agent_traits: (same pattern as user_kb)
                            │
                            ├─ user_traits:
                            │     SQLite SELECT → merge deltas → SQLite UPDATE
                            │     (no Qdrant)
                            │
                            └─ Reset MTM segment l_interaction = 0
```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (heavy), Qdrant (for KB + agent_traits), LLM/embed API.
- **Eviction**: pure FIFO on `created_at`. No heat.
- **Tightly coupled to MTM**: this loop only runs because MTM said so.

---

# Cross-Loop Summary Table

| Event | STM | MTM | LPM | DBs touched |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| User sends msg | INSERT page | retrieved (read) | retrieved (read) | SQLite, Qdrant |
| STM > 7 | DELETE oldest | INSERT page, possibly new segment | — | SQLite, Qdrant, Redis |
| Segment grows | — | UPDATE l_interaction, possibly re-summarize | — | SQLite, Qdrant |
| Segment retrieved | — | UPDATE n_visit, last_access_time | — | SQLite |
| Segment hot (>5) | — | UPDATE l_interaction = 0 | INSERT facts/traits, FIFO trim | SQLite, Qdrant |
| MTM > 200 | — | DELETE coldest segment + its pages | — | SQLite, Qdrant |
| KB > 100 | — | — | DELETE oldest by created_at | SQLite, Qdrant |

---

# Heat Lifecycle in One Diagram

```
Page joins segment ─► l_interaction++   ┐
Segment retrieved  ─► n_visit++         │
                      last_access_time   │   inputs that drive heat
Time passes        ─► R_recency decays  ┘

Heat = n_visit + l_interaction + R_recency  (computed, never stored)

Heat crosses 5 ─► lpm-promote ─► l_interaction = 0 (heat drops)
Heat lowest    ─► mtm-evict   ─► segment + pages gone (SQLite + Qdrant)
```

This is the entire heat machinery. Two threshold checks (`> 5` and `lowest`) drive both egress paths from MTM.

That's all three loops, every DB touch, every counter, every trigger.

```md
# The Three Memory Loops — End to End

A "loop" here = the **full lifecycle** of memory at that tier: ingress (data coming in), in-place updates (heat, counters, summaries), and egress (data leaving via promotion or eviction). Each loop touches specific DBs in a specific order.

---

# LOOP 1: STM Loop (Short-Term Memory)

**Scope**: A bounded queue of 7 Q&A pages per user. Lives entirely in **Bun SQLite** (no Qdrant, no Redis state). The fastest, simplest loop.

## A. Ingress — New page enters STM

**Trigger**: User sends a chat message and gets a response.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Insert new page (query, response, timestamp) | SQLite | `INSERT INTO stm_pages` |
| 2 | Check current count for this user | SQLite | `SELECT COUNT(*)` |
| 3 | If count > 7, identify oldest page | SQLite | `SELECT ... ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 1` |
| 4 | Enqueue chain-summarize job | Redis (BullMQ) | `queue.add('chain-summarize', {page_id})` |
| 5 | If overflow, enqueue stm-migrate | Redis (BullMQ) | `queue.add('stm-migrate', {page_id})` |

**At this moment**: STM has 7 or 8 entries. The oldest is "in flight" toward MTM.

## B. In-place update — `chain-summarize` runs (async)

**Trigger**: BullMQ worker picks up the `chain-summarize` job.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load new page + last 3 STM pages | SQLite | `SELECT ... ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 4` |
| 2 | LLM call: is new page continuous with prior chain? | (external LLM) | API call |
| 3 | Decide `chain_id`: reuse existing or generate new | (in-app) | UUID logic |
| 4 | LLM call: summarize the chain | (external LLM) | API call |
| 5 | Update the page row | SQLite | `UPDATE stm_pages SET chain_id=?, meta_chain=?` |

**No Qdrant interaction in STM. Ever.** STM is fully retrieved as a list, not searched semantically.

## C. Egress — STM → MTM (handled by `stm-migrate` job — described in Loop 2 below)

The oldest page **leaves STM only when migrated**. The `stm-migrate` worker:
1. Inserts the page into MTM (SQLite + Qdrant).
2. Deletes it from STM (SQLite).

So STM stays bounded at ≤7 within a few seconds of going over.

## D. STM retrieval (read path)

**Trigger**: Every user request.

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM stm_pages WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 7` |

No scoring, no Jaccard, no embeddings. Just dump the queue into the prompt.

## STM loop summary

\```
[User msg] → SQLite.INSERT → Redis.queue(chain-summarize, stm-migrate)
                                       │
                                       ├─ worker → LLM → SQLite.UPDATE (meta_chain)
                                       │
                                       └─ worker → kicks off MTM loop
\```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (always), Redis (always for queue), LLM API (background).
- **No Qdrant at all.**
- **No heat logic at all.**
- Hardest invariant: STM size ≤ 7 (enforced by checking on each insert).

---

# LOOP 2: MTM Loop (Mid-Term Memory)

**Scope**: Up to 200 segments per user, each containing many pages. This is where **Qdrant and SQLite work together** and where **heat-based logic kicks in**.

## A. Ingress — Page arrives from STM

**Trigger**: `stm-migrate` worker picks up a job.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load the migrating page from SQLite | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id=?` |
| 2 | Embed page content (query + response) | (external embed API) | API call |
| 3 | LLM-extract keywords | (external LLM) | API call |
| 4 | Find candidate segments | **Qdrant** | `search(mtm_segments, vector=query_emb, filter=user_id, limit=10)` |
| 5 | Pull candidate keyword sets for Jaccard | SQLite | `SELECT keywords FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id IN (...)` |
| 6 | Compute F_score for each (cosine + Jaccard) | (in-app) | math |
| 7a | If max F_score > 0.6: assign to that segment | — | branch |
| 7b | Else: create a new segment | — | branch |

### Path 7a — Joining existing segment

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_pages (segment_id, ...)` |
| 2 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = l_interaction + 1, last_access_time = NOW()` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_pages, {id, vector, payload={user_id, segment_id, keywords}})` |
| 4 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM stm_pages WHERE page_id=?` |
| 5 | Redis | `queue.add('segment-update', {segment_id})` |
| 6 | Redis | `queue.add('heat-check', {segment_id})` |

### Path 7b — Creating new segment

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | (LLM) | Generate initial segment summary from the page |
| 2 | (embed API) | Embed the new summary |
| 3 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_segments (segment_id, summary, keywords, n_visit=0, l_interaction=1, last_access_time=NOW())` |
| 4 | SQLite | `INSERT INTO mtm_pages (segment_id, ...)` |
| 5 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_segments, {id, vector=summary_emb, payload})` |
| 6 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_pages, {id, vector=page_emb, payload})` |
| 7 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM stm_pages` |
| 8 | Redis | `queue.add('heat-check', {segment_id})` |

## B. In-place updates inside MTM

Three things continuously change while a segment lives in MTM:

### B1. Counter: `l_interaction`

- **What**: Number of pages in segment.
- **When updated**: Every time a new page joins (path 7a or 7b).
- **DB**: SQLite — `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = l_interaction + 1`.

### B2. Counter: `n_visit` + `last_access_time`

- **What**: Retrieval popularity + recency.
- **When updated**: Every time a segment is retrieved during `/chat`.
- **DB**: SQLite — batched UPDATE during the request handler.
  \```sql
  UPDATE mtm_segments
  SET n_visit = n_visit + 1, last_access_time = ?
  WHERE segment_id IN (...);
  \```

### B3. Summary + keywords (re-summarization)

- **What**: The segment's summary text and keyword set, used for retrieval matching.
- **When updated**: `segment-update` job, triggered when a new page joined.
- **Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Load segment + all pages |
| 2 | (LLM) | Regenerate summary covering all pages |
| 3 | (LLM) | Merge/update keyword set |
| 4 | (embed API) | Re-embed new summary |
| 5 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET summary=?, keywords=?` |
| 6 | Qdrant | `upsert(mtm_segments, {id, vector=new_emb, payload={keywords}})` |

## C. Heat lifecycle (the core of the MTM loop)

**Heat is never stored** — it's computed from three inputs that ARE stored.

### Heat inputs (stored in SQLite per segment)

| Field | Source | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `n_visit` | incremented on retrieval | int |
| `l_interaction` | incremented on page-join, reset on promotion | int |
| `last_access_time` | refreshed on retrieval or new page-join | timestamp |

### Heat formula (computed on read)

\```
dt = (now - last_access_time) / 1000      // seconds
R_recency = exp(-dt / 1e7)                // 0 to 1
Heat = n_visit + l_interaction + R_recency
\```

Or directly in SQL:

\```sql
SELECT segment_id,
  n_visit + l_interaction + exp((last_access_time - ?) / 1e10) AS heat
FROM mtm_segments
WHERE user_id = ?;
\```

### When heat is evaluated — `heat-check` job

Triggered after any `stm-migrate` or `segment-update`.

**Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Load segment counters |
| 2 | (in-app) | Compute heat |
| 3 | SQLite | Count user's total segments |
| 4 | (branch) | If heat > 5 → `queue.add('lpm-promote', ...)` |
| 5 | (branch) | If count > 200 → `queue.add('mtm-evict', ...)` |

No DB writes in this job. It's purely a router.

## D. Egress — Two ways out of MTM

### D1. Promotion (heat > 5) → `lpm-promote` job

Detailed in Loop 3. The key MTM-side effect:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| End | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id=?` |

**Why reset?** If we didn't, the segment would stay above heat=5 forever. Reset drops heat, segment must "earn it again."

The segment itself stays in MTM. Only the **derived persona data** moves to LPM.

### D2. Eviction (MTM > 200 segments) → `mtm-evict` job

**Sequence**:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Find lowest-heat segment via heat-expression ORDER BY ASC LIMIT 1 |
| 2 | SQLite | `BEGIN; DELETE FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id=?; DELETE FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id=?; COMMIT;` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `delete(mtm_pages, filter={segment_id})` |
| 4 | Qdrant | `delete(mtm_segments, points=[segment_id])` |

> Note: anything that already promoted to LPM remains. Eviction only loses the working-zone copy.

## E. MTM retrieval (read path)

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Qdrant | `search(mtm_segments, vector, filter=user_id, limit=10)` |
| 2 | SQLite | `SELECT keywords FROM mtm_segments WHERE segment_id IN (...)` |
| 3 | (in-app) | Compute Jaccard + F_score, pick top-5 |
| 4 | Qdrant | `search(mtm_pages, vector, filter={user_id, segment_id IN top-5}, limit=10)` |
| 5 | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM mtm_pages WHERE page_id IN (...)` |
| 6 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET n_visit = n_visit + 1, last_access_time = ? WHERE segment_id IN (top-5)` |

Heat inputs get bumped automatically on every retrieval.

## MTM loop summary

\```
[from STM] ──► stm-migrate worker
                  │
                  ├─ embed page (API)
                  ├─ Qdrant search candidates
                  ├─ SQLite load keywords
                  ├─ F_score → assign or create segment
                  ├─ SQLite insert page + update segment counters
                  ├─ Qdrant upsert page (+ segment if new)
                  ├─ SQLite delete from STM
                  └─► segment-update + heat-check
                          │
                          ├─ segment-update: LLM resummarize → SQLite + Qdrant update
                          └─ heat-check: compute heat
                                ├─ heat > 5 → lpm-promote
                                └─ count > 200 → mtm-evict
\```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (heavy), Qdrant (heavy), Redis queue (always), LLM/embed API (background only).
- **Heat fields updated**: on retrieval (n_visit, last_access_time) and on page-join (l_interaction).
- **Heat reset**: only on LPM promotion (l_interaction → 0).

---

# LOOP 3: LPM Loop (Long-term Persona Memory)

**Scope**: Per-user persistent persona. 5 sub-stores. Mix of static and dynamic. The slowest loop — only fires when an MTM segment is hot enough.

## LPM has 5 stores; treat each separately

| Store | DB | Capacity | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user_profile` | SQLite | 1 row | Static |
| `agent_profile` | SQLite | 1 row | Static |
| `user_traits` | SQLite | 1 row (JSON of 90 floats) | Dynamic (merged) |
| `user_kb` | SQLite + Qdrant | FIFO 100 entries | Dynamic (FIFO eviction) |
| `agent_traits` | SQLite + Qdrant | FIFO 100 entries | Dynamic (FIFO eviction) |

## A. Ingress — `lpm-promote` job runs

**Trigger**: `heat-check` decided a segment's heat > 5.

**Sequence**:

| Step | Action | DB touched | Operation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Load segment + all pages | SQLite | `SELECT * FROM mtm_pages WHERE segment_id=?` |
| 2 | LLM call: extract user facts | (LLM) | API call |
| 3 | LLM call: extract agent behavior facts | (LLM) | API call |
| 4 | LLM call: produce trait deltas for 90 dims | (LLM) | API call |

(Can be combined into 1–2 calls to save cost.)

### Writes for User KB

For each new user fact:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 5a | SQLite | `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO user_kb (entry_id, user_id, fact, created_at)` (dedup by hash) |
| 5b | (embed API) | Embed the fact |
| 5c | Qdrant | `upsert(lpm_user_kb, {id=entry_id, vector, payload={user_id}})` |

Then FIFO eviction:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 6a | SQLite | Find entries beyond the 100 newest |
| 6b | SQLite | `DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE entry_id IN (...)` |
| 6c | Qdrant | `delete(lpm_user_kb, points=[evicted_ids])` |

### Writes for Agent Traits

Same pattern as User KB — SQLite insert + Qdrant upsert + FIFO trim in both.

### Writes for User Traits

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 7a | SQLite | `SELECT traits FROM user_traits WHERE user_id=?` → parse JSON |
| 7b | (in-app) | Merge LLM-produced deltas into the 90-dim object |
| 7c | SQLite | `UPDATE user_traits SET traits=? WHERE user_id=?` |

User Traits is **not** stored in Qdrant — there's no top-10 search on it.

### Final step — reset segment heat

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 8 | SQLite | `UPDATE mtm_segments SET l_interaction = 0 WHERE segment_id=?` |

This **closes the LPM-promotion loop** by cooling the source segment.

## B. In-place updates inside LPM

**User Profile / Agent Profile (static)**: never updated by background loops. Only via explicit user/admin action.

**User Traits**: updated only during `lpm-promote` jobs (Step 7 above).

**User KB / Agent Traits**: appended during promotion. FIFO trim happens within the same job.

## C. Egress — FIFO eviction

When `user_kb` or `agent_traits` exceeds 100 entries:

| Step | DB | Operation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | SQLite | Get IDs of entries beyond the newest 100 |
| 2 | SQLite | `DELETE FROM user_kb WHERE entry_id NOT IN (SELECT entry_id FROM user_kb WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100)` |
| 3 | Qdrant | `delete(lpm_user_kb, points=[evicted_ids])` |

No heat involved. Pure FIFO by `created_at`.

## D. LPM retrieval (read path)

Run in parallel during the request:

| Sub-store | Strategy | DBs |
|---|---|---|
| `user_profile` | Full row | SQLite |
| `agent_profile` | Full row | SQLite |
| `user_traits` | Full 90-dim JSON | SQLite |
| `user_kb` | Top-10 semantic | Qdrant search → SQLite hydrate |
| `agent_traits` | Top-10 semantic | Qdrant search → SQLite hydrate |

All wrapped in a `Promise.all`.

## LPM loop summary

\```
[from MTM heat-check] ──► lpm-promote worker
                            │
                            ├─ LLM extract: user facts, agent traits, trait deltas
                            │
                            ├─ user_kb:
                            │     SQLite INSERT
                            │     embed + Qdrant upsert
                            │     SQLite FIFO trim → Qdrant delete
                            │
                            ├─ agent_traits: (same pattern as user_kb)
                            │
                            ├─ user_traits:
                            │     SQLite SELECT → merge deltas → SQLite UPDATE
                            │     (no Qdrant)
                            │
                            └─ Reset MTM segment l_interaction = 0
\```

- **DBs touched**: SQLite (heavy), Qdrant (for KB + agent_traits), LLM/embed API.
- **Eviction**: pure FIFO on `created_at`. No heat.
- **Tightly coupled to MTM**: this loop only runs because MTM said so.

---

# Cross-Loop Summary Table

| Event | STM | MTM | LPM | DBs touched |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| User sends msg | INSERT page | retrieved (read) | retrieved (read) | SQLite, Qdrant |
| STM > 7 | DELETE oldest | INSERT page, possibly new segment | — | SQLite, Qdrant, Redis |
| Segment grows | — | UPDATE l_interaction, possibly re-summarize | — | SQLite, Qdrant |
| Segment retrieved | — | UPDATE n_visit, last_access_time | — | SQLite |
| Segment hot (>5) | — | UPDATE l_interaction = 0 | INSERT facts/traits, FIFO trim | SQLite, Qdrant |
| MTM > 200 | — | DELETE coldest segment + its pages | — | SQLite, Qdrant |
| KB > 100 | — | — | DELETE oldest by created_at | SQLite, Qdrant |

---

# Heat Lifecycle in One Diagram

\```
Page joins segment ─► l_interaction++   ┐
Segment retrieved  ─► n_visit++         │
                      last_access_time   │   inputs that drive heat
Time passes        ─► R_recency decays  ┘

Heat = n_visit + l_interaction + R_recency  (computed, never stored)

Heat crosses 5 ─► lpm-promote ─► l_interaction = 0 (heat drops)
Heat lowest    ─► mtm-evict   ─► segment + pages gone (SQLite + Qdrant)
\```

This is the entire heat machinery. Two threshold checks (`> 5` and `lowest`) drive both egress paths from MTM.
